Functions to calculate CRC32 for byte streams and flash memory, not safe for ISR calls.

# Purpose
This C header file provides declarations for two functions that calculate the CRC32 checksum for a stream of bytes. The function [`crc_calculate_bytes`](<#crc_calculate_bytes>) computes the CRC32 for data provided as a pointer to a byte array and its length. The function [`crc_calculate_flash`](<#crc_calculate_flash>) calculates the CRC32 for data stored in flash memory, given a starting address and the number of bytes to process. Both functions are not safe to call from an Interrupt Service Routine (ISR). The file includes the standard library header `<stdint.h>` to use fixed-width integer types.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`
